steps.title: Set up the mixture equation
steps.content:
The mixture contains only vitamin C and magnesium. Since these are the only two components, their masses must sum to the total mass:
$$\text{vitamin C mass} + \text{magnesium mass} = \text{total mass}$$

steps.title: Solve for magnesium mass
steps.content:
Substitute the known values into the equation:
$$60 + \text{magnesium mass} = 180$$
Subtract 60 from both sides:
$$\text{magnesium mass} = 180 - 60 = 120$$
That is the correct answer.
